subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – Wisdom Paves the Way
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Birbal was one of the wisest ministers in the court of Emperor Akbar. He was respected not only for his intelligence but also for his fairness and calm thinking. Whenever the emperor faced a difficult situation, Birbal was often the person who found the most sensible solution.
questions: Who was Birbal?
In whose court did Birbal serve?
What qualities was Birbal respected for?
Who usually solved difficult situations in the court?
Why did the emperor trust Birbal?

passages.paragraph: Emperor Akbar believed that wisdom was more powerful than strength. He liked to test the intelligence of his ministers through puzzles and questions. Among all the courtiers, Birbal often impressed the emperor with his clever and thoughtful answers.
questions: What did Emperor Akbar believe about wisdom?
How did the emperor test his ministers?
Who impressed the emperor the most?
What kind of answers did Birbal give?
Why did Akbar enjoy testing his ministers?

passages.paragraph: One day, a traveler arrived at the royal court with a complaint. He claimed that another man had cheated him during a business deal. Both men argued loudly, each insisting that he was telling the truth.
questions: Who arrived at the royal court?
What was the traveler’s complaint?
Who was accused of cheating?
How did the two men behave in the court?
What did each man claim?

passages.paragraph: The emperor listened carefully to both sides but found it difficult to decide who was telling the truth. The situation confused many of the courtiers. Finally, Emperor Akbar asked Birbal to investigate the matter.
questions: What did the emperor do first?
Why was it difficult for the emperor to decide?
How did the courtiers feel about the situation?
Whom did the emperor ask for help?
What task was given to Birbal?

passages.paragraph: Birbal examined the situation calmly and asked both men several questions. Instead of rushing to a judgment, he carefully observed their behavior and the way they answered his questions.
questions: How did Birbal approach the problem?
What did he ask the two men?
Did Birbal hurry to give a decision?
What did Birbal observe carefully?
Why did Birbal ask several questions?

passages.paragraph: After listening to their replies, Birbal suggested a clever test to discover the truth. He believed that honest people remain calm, while those who lie often become nervous when tested.
questions: What did Birbal suggest after hearing their replies?
Why did he suggest a test?
What did Birbal believe about honest people?
How do dishonest people usually behave?
What was Birbal trying to discover?

passages.paragraph: During the test, one of the men became anxious and confused. His answers changed several times, which made the courtiers suspicious. Birbal quietly noticed these changes in behavior.
questions: What happened during the test?
How did one of the men behave?
What made the courtiers suspicious?
What did Birbal observe quietly?
Why were the changing answers important?

passages.paragraph: Birbal then explained his observations to the emperor. He pointed out the contradictions in the man’s statements. Through logical reasoning, Birbal showed who was truly responsible for the cheating.
questions: What did Birbal explain to the emperor?
What did he point out in the man’s statements?
What method did Birbal use to reveal the truth?
What did Birbal prove?
Why was Birbal’s explanation convincing?

passages.paragraph: Emperor Akbar praised Birbal for solving the problem wisely. The court admired his patience and careful thinking. The innocent traveler finally received justice because of Birbal’s intelligence.
questions: How did the emperor react to Birbal’s solution?
What qualities of Birbal did the court admire?
Who received justice in the end?
Why did the traveler receive justice?
How did Birbal’s intelligence help solve the case?

passages.paragraph: The story reminds us that wisdom, patience, and careful observation can solve even complicated problems. True intelligence lies not in quick decisions but in thoughtful understanding and fair judgment.
questions: What lesson does the story teach?
Which qualities help solve complicated problems?
What does true intelligence involve?
Why is patience important in decision-making?
How does careful observation help in solving problems?
